calorieDeficit.title: Calorie Deficit Calculator
calorieDeficit.description: Calculate your daily calorie goal for weight loss — based on the Mifflin-St Jeor formula.
calorieDeficit.warningUnsafe: Your calorie goal is below 1,200 kcal/day. This is a health concern — please consult a doctor.
calorieDeficit.warningAggressive: You are planning to lose more than 1 kg per week. A slower pace is more sustainable and healthier.

faq.q: What is a healthy calorie deficit?
faq.a: 300–500 kcal/day below TDEE produces roughly 0.3–0.5 kg of fat loss per week. This pace is considered sustainable by major nutrition bodies.

faq.q: What is the minimum I should eat?
faq.a: Women should not chronically eat below 1,200 kcal/day, men below 1,500 kcal/day. Lower intakes risk nutrient deficiencies and metabolic adaptation.

faq.q: Why is 7,000 kcal deficit ≠ 1 kg of fat?
faq.a: The '3,500 kcal = 1 lb fat' rule is a simplification. Water shifts, glycogen depletion, and muscle changes affect the scale. Actual fat loss is usually 0.3–0.5 kg per 3,500 kcal deficit.

faq.q: How do I avoid muscle loss while dieting?
faq.a: Eating 1.6–2.2 g of protein per kg of body weight and resistance training 2–4×/week sharply reduces muscle loss (ISSN guidance).

faq.q: When should I take a diet break?
faq.a: After 8–12 weeks in a deficit, a 7–14-day diet break at maintenance calories can help restore hormones (leptin) and metabolic rate.

faq.q: What do I do if I plateau?
faq.a: Plateaus are normal. Re-check tracking accuracy, raise activity, lower calories by ~100 kcal, or take a brief diet break.
